subject: Now is the time for USMCA
body:
Yesterday, America’s leading manufacturing group upped the pressure on Congress to act | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0206ef9f65&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 21, 2019
---
| | |
# Now is the time for USMCA****

**Last November,** President Donald J. Trump fulfilled one of his most important promises to voters when he joined leaders from both Canada and Mexico to sign the United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA), replacing the outdated NAFTA.

**“In short** , this is a model agreement that changes the trade landscape forever. And this is an agreement that, first and foremost, benefits working people,” the President said.

**Reps. Kevin Brady (R-TX) and Steve Scalise (R-LA)** explain that “the U.S. will be losing out on more jobs, more customers for Made-in-America goods and a stronger economy unless the U.S. House of Representatives takes up the updated trade agreement with Mexico and Canada.”

**Yesterday** , America’s leading manufacturing group upped the pressure on Congress to act. “Passage of this agreement is critical for U.S. manufacturing sector,” said Linda Dempsey, the National Association of Manufacturers’ (NAM) vice president of international economic affairs. “Canada and Mexico are manufacturers’ most important partners.”

**“USMCA passage is critical** for manufacturers in every state,” according to a new [comprehensive, state-by-state analysis](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f7a969d4ef&e=3ee4985eea) by NAM. “Canada and Mexico purchase one-fifth of the total value of U.S. manufacturing output – more than the next 11 countries combined,” NAM’s Andrew Clark writes. “These exports support about 2 million American manufacturing jobs and 43,000 small- and medium-sized businesses.”

**Vice President Mike Pence** told workers in Jacksonville, Florida, yesterday that “we are not going to allow an outdated trade deal to hurt American manufacturers or American farmers anymore.” Under NAFTA, nations like China were able to game the system, shipping their products to Canada or Mexico—and then sending them into the United States duty-free. “Those days are over,” the Vice President said.

“**We’re not going to let them** undermine good-paying jobs that build the . . . American middle class in this country, or the middle class I grew up in.”

[Reps. Kevin Brady (R-TX) and Steve Scalise (R-LA): “We must pass USMCA now.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d257f76db5&e=3ee4985eea)

**In the headlines:** [USMCA will boost the American economy and create jobs](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=228cb55b49&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump hosts the Freely Associated States**

**President Trump** welcomed the presidents of the Republic of Palau, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Federated States of Micronesia to the Oval Office today—the first time all three leaders from the Freely Associated States have been hosted together at the White House by an American President.

**In****a joint statement** with President Trump, the three leaders reaffirmed their common interest in a free, open, and prosperous Indo-Pacific region.

**America shares** a special relationship with these nations, dating back to our shared sacrifice during World War II. This partnership didn’t end with the war. Today, citizens from the Freely Associated States are permitted to serve in the U.S. military, with several hundred currently on active duty.

**“We are confident** our relationships will further our abiding mutual interests and remain a source of regional security, stability, and prosperity,” the four leaders said.

[Read the statement from President Trump and leaders of the Freely Associated States. ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0aebe54152&e=3ee4985eea)

| Heads up, friend: Donald Trump doesn't like what I said about him yesterday. Not one bit.

Yesterday, I gave a speech about my foreign policy and national security priorities, and when it came to Trump, I didn't hold back. I called him out for what he is: a willing Russian puppet, an isolationist, and a bully who has lowered our standing in the world.

**Those words hit a nerve --and just a few hours later, Donald Trump's campaign hit back**, releasing a laughable statement claiming that the Trump sanctions on Russia are "the toughest," that Trump "decimated" ISIS, and other bits of empty bluster and bravado. In short, typical Trump.

**Friend, we plan to keep Donald Trump on defense until we run him right out of the White House. Help us do it --rush a donation now to help us keep the pressure on and win.** _If you've saved your information with ActBlue Express your donation will go through right away._ [**_Donate $5 now_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771628/691c9bd/6c002cac/11a4fcf9/1889144727/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-security?refcode=em_fr_20190521_JH_20190521-fr-JH-trumpbump-all_all_express5&amount=5&expresslane=true&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

[** _Donate $50 now_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771628/691c9bd/6c002cac/11a4fcf4/1889144727/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-security?refcode=em_fr_20190521_JH_20190521-fr-JH-trumpbump-all_all_express50&amount=50&expresslane=true&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

[** _Donate $100 now_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771628/691c9bd/6c002cac/11a4fcf5/1889144727/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-security?refcode=em_fr_20190521_JH_20190521-fr-JH-trumpbump-all_all_express100&amount=100&expresslane=true&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") The Trump campaign's attempt at hitting back shows me they see the substance and integrity that our campaign is bringing to this race and to the challenges facing our country--and they know Donald Trump can't handle it. I'm ready to handle that bully--on a debate stage, in a general election, and anywhere else I need to.

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: here's how we protect Roe v. Wade
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hey there,
The GOP's uncompromising bans on women's reproductive rights that are moving forward in Alabama, Missouri, and other states are shaking me to my very core. I expect that's the case for many people.
One of the best ways we can stop Republican politicians from undermining women's freedom and stacking the courts with right-wing ideologues is to make sure that more pro-choice Democratic women are at the decision-making table.
That's where MJ Hegar comes in. She's running for U.S. Senate in Texas and if she wins, she'll unseat an anti-choice Republican, John Cornyn.
**MJ 's race will be tough, but it's winnable so long as she has the funds to power her campaign. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Indiana Republican Party
body:
This election year, we're working to win Indiana's cities and towns with Republican candidates across our state who will deliver results at the... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9bb5cfb87b)

| Dear Indiana Republicans,

This election year, we're working to win Indiana's cities and towns with Republican candidates across our state who will deliver results at the local level for Hoosiers.

And now following the primary election, we need to skill-up candidates and party officials, helping our candidates and local campaign teams build the grassroots, finance, and communications skills needed to get Republicans across the finish line FIRST on election night.

If you're running for office, thinking about running soon, or working on a campaign, [secure your ticket now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ee78d567a7&e=1acd9bb3d6) for our full-day candidate training seminar, **Battleground Indiana: Winning Indiana's Cities & Towns**. [The seminar](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a8ba636131&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- designed with our 2019 Republican municipal candidates and campaigns in mind -- will be on **Saturday, June 15, at the University of Indianapolis. **

At the seminar, we'll bring in top experts who will teach you how to:
* Determine your winning coalition
* Recruit and organize volunteers
* Fundraise successfully
* Communicate with voters using new technology
* [And MORE!](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=82ca344d27&e=1acd9bb3d6)

[Your registration](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5104e1df0b&e=1acd9bb3d6) includes everything that you'll need for the day -- breakfast, lunch, materials and training sessions. You'll also receive admission to our special mayoral candidate reception that evening headlined by Governor Eric Holcomb and me, with all funds raised during the reception directly supporting our 2019 municipal efforts.

We're expecting this to be a packed event, so [make sure you secure your ticket ASAP](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c89f1bb5ab&e=1acd9bb3d6) for this important training seminar!

If you have any questions, please reach out at [info@indiana.gop](mailto:info@indiana.gop), and we'll be in touch soon.

Thanks,

Kyle Hupfer
Chairman, Indiana Republican Party

P.S. Know someone who is a candidate? Or who's thinking about running soon? Forward this email, and encourage them to register for this [information-packed seminar](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a6acf7cfa0&e=1acd9bb3d6). We want to make sure every 2019 Republican has the opportunity to skill-up to WIN in this year's races.

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Peter, let me introduce you to these folks:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
| | Today, Abigail Spanberger is joining forces with eight other Democrats who have service backgrounds to help them fight back against Washington special interests.

[**Will you fight alongside Abigail and these new Representatives by splitting a donation of $9, one dollar to each of them, right now?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/PYgsGRxXyCETnNIswOLcXaz1i-7jIi1Xn4vAco47IyY=/rCuv2UgTME1zT1wLbM7a6yLvBwKC4QJbj3mTN4x09cU=?refcode=abigailspanberger&amount=9&recurring=auto) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/PYgsGRxXyCETnNIswOLcXaz1i-7jIi1Xn4vAco47IyY=/rCuv2UgTME1zT1wLbM7a6yLvBwKC4QJbj3mTN4x09cU=?refcode=abigailspanberger&amount=9&recurring=auto)
---
* * *
Peter,

If you've served, you know there's a siblinghood that quickly develops. You have the back of the people who fight next to you and they have yours. And you also learn what it means to be a servant leader -- someone who puts the needs of others before their own.

In Congress, I'm proud to serve alongside and have been elected with, eight other Democrats who represent a new generation of leadership. We may have served in different ways, but we are united in our mission to fight for regular Americans, not the special interests.
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/PYgsGRxXyCETnNIswOLcXaz1i-7jIi1Xn4vAco47IyY=/rCuv2UgTME1zT1wLbM7a6yLvBwKC4QJbj3mTN4x09cU=?refcode=abigailspanberger&amount=9&recurring=auto)
Whether it's campaign finance reform, greater access to affordable health care, or battling climate change, we're in this fight together.

And we have another thing in common: _none of our seats are safe from Super PACs who will stop at nothing to keep us from getting re-elected._

[**So, I 'm joining forces with my fellow Representatives to make sure we all have each other's back in 2020. Can we count on you to chip in $9, a dollar for each of us, to help us all prepare to fight back?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/PYgsGRxXyCETnNIswOLcXaz1i-7jIi1Xn4vAco47IyY=/rCuv2UgTME1zT1wLbM7a6yLvBwKC4QJbj3mTN4x09cU=?refcode=abigailspanberger&amount=9&recurring=auto)
We all know the meaning of service and sacrifice.

Each and every one of us served alongside Americans from every walk of life who sacrificed for each other every day. Democrat or Republican, it didn't matter; we were Americans first and our commitment to each other was more important than any of our differences.

But over the last two-plus years, we've seen what happens when out-of-touch leaders continue to push for the same old solutions to problems that are changing faster than ever -- and opt to serve the special interests instead of the American people.

[**Now more than ever, we need leaders who will put country before politics and who understand what it means to serve. So please, chip in $9 or more now to help us continue to fight for you in Washington.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/PYgsGRxXyCETnNIswOLcXaz1i-7jIi1Xn4vAco47IyY=/rCuv2UgTME1zT1wLbM7a6yLvBwKC4QJbj3mTN4x09cU=?refcode=abigailspanberger&amount=9&recurring=auto)

---
Thank you,

Abigail

She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. clean_body: | | Today, Abigail Spanberger is joining forces with eight other Democrats who have service backgrounds to help them fight back against Washington special interests. | * Peter, If you've served, you know there's a siblinghood that quickly develops. You have the back of the people who fight next to you and they have yours. And you also learn what it means to be a servant leader -- someone who puts the needs of others before their own. In Congress, I'm proud to serve alongside and have been elected with, eight other Democrats who represent a new generation of leadership. We may have served in different ways, but we are united in our mission to fight for regular Americans, not the special interests. Whether it's campaign finance reform, greater access to affordable health care, or battling climate change, we're in this fight together. And we have another thing in common: none of our seats are safe from Super PACs who will stop at nothing to keep us from getting re-elected. We all know the meaning of service and sacrifice. Each and every one of us served alongside Americans from every walk of life who sacrificed for each other every day. Democrat or Republican, it didn't matter; we were Americans first and our commitment to each other was more important than any of our differences. But over the last two-plus years, we've seen what happens when out-of-touch leaders continue to push for the same old solutions to problems that are changing faster than ever -- and opt to serve the special interests instead of the American people. Thank you, Abigail | Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. name: Andrew Yang
subject: When I'm President, We Will Have Clean Drinking Water. That's For Sure.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05222019_newsletter_2&n=1)[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05222019_newsletter_2&n=2) Hello and thank you for the support! It means a ton to me and the team. I spent the past weekend in South Carolina and had the opportunity to meet with hundreds of amazing, hard-working Americans. And, like all Americans, they are worried about the future for their families. If you’re like me, you associate drinking water issues in America with Flint, Michigan. But that's just the most visible of dozens of communities around the country that are struggling with their water supply. In Denmark, South Carolina, a chemical not approved by the EPA was used for a decade to decontaminate the water, leading to health issues for residents there. Imagine having kids who have been drinking water for years and now knowing that their health might be damaged as a result. As our infrastructure falls into disrepair and there is less and less money to fix it, these problems will only get worse. The EPA has estimated that it would cost $384 billion over a ten-year period to properly fix and maintain our water systems. We live in the wealthiest nation in the world, and we must be able to guarantee clean drinking water for each of our citizens. When I’m President, we will invest the resources needed to guarantee clean drinking water to all Americans as a basic right. South Carolina recently made national news because the schoolteachers there went on strike for better pay and benefits. They rank 38th nationwide on teacher pay. Teachers are vital to our future, and when teachers aren’t able to make a decent living, we lose the best of them to other opportunities. When I’m President, I plan to make sure teachers get paid more—the data shows that a good teacher is worth their weight in gold in terms of driving better educational outcomes for dozens of students each year. Instead of nickel-and-diming teachers, we should be treating them like professionals and giving them the flexibility to do their jobs. While increasing teacher pay is important, making things better in the classroom is just part of the fight. Only 25-30% of our kids’ performance in schools is attributable to what happens in school. The rest of it is due to factors outside of school—parental time and income, number of words read to a child, stress levels, type of neighborhood, etc. Educators know this. We are putting them in an impossible situation where we expect them to educate our children, who are distracted by issues at home. The biggest thing we can do to set teachers up for success is to get the Freedom Dividend into the hands of all Americans so that our families are stronger. [**That’s why I’m running. Because we need to start putting forward real solutions that will directly improve people’s lives. I need you to help me make these solutions a reality. If you can, please donate $10 to our movement. I genuinely appreciate it.**](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztwOurmyDQdaH3NaKLwEpcO18xGzef5TvO42JaBQed0jXS7z6B0vQBSFYMqiqGORLM&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05222019_newsletter_2&n=3 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang") Each time I visit a new state, I’m more certain that what we are doing is vital to our nation’s future. There are so many Americans out there who are struggling to make ends meet. Let’s invest in our people, our families, and our communities so that we can build a better future together. We do not have unlimited time. Your grateful candidate, -Andrew
---

Most of our donations come from people like you chipping in small amounts when they can. Your continued generosity helps us invest in people.

**Join the Yang Gang!**

body:

| Team,

When I announced the Green New Deal resolution with Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, I knew we were at the start of something that would transform our democracy and our economy.

The Green New Deal is a revolution--one that is putting fear in the hearts of the fossil fuel industry across the country because people are taking notice, speaking up, and demanding change.

In the face of the opposition, in the face of climate-denier-in-chief Donald Trump, in the face of critics and naysayers, tens of thousands of people, young and old, are organizing to save all of creation through massive job creation.

**That 's what the Green New Deal will do, and that's what I'm fighting for every day.**

But I also knew that Republicans and the right-wing media would go CRAZY. And crazy they went.

They spread outright lies about me and the Green New Deal resolution, formed super PACs to launch attack ads, and pulled together dark-money groups to spread disinformation.

Now, they're scoping out extremist candidates to defeat me and be Donald Trump's standard bearer here in Massachusetts who will cater to Big Oil and fossil fuel interests.

**That's why we MUST show we have the grassroots strength to fight and win. We set a goal to reach 500 new 2019 Members of my campaign before midnight Friday. Can I count on you to be a part of this exclusive team?**

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Americans Feel Good About Economy and Give Trump Credit, CBS News Poll Says
body:
"Seven in 10 Americans say the economy is in good shape, including a quarter who say it is very good." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bbd89b5c28&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Americans Feel Good About Economy and Give Trump Credit, CBS News Poll Says**

“Most Americans remain confident about the U.S. economy and in their own financial situation,” CBS News reports. “Seven in 10 Americans say the economy is in good shape, including a quarter who say it is very good. Nearly all Republicans and most Democrats and independents share this view.”

The results under President Trump have drowned out the media and political spin about his Presidency: Today, 85 percent of Americans who rate the economy as good said the President’s policies are mostly or at least partially responsible for it.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ce91f72eed&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Democrats are fuming that Don McGahn skipped a Congressional hearing on Tuesday . . . But Mr. McGahn has every constitutional right not to appear, and this isn’t a close legal call,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e5c54ab08d&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. House Judiciary Chairman Jerry Nadler (D-NY) “doesn’t expect to learn anything new. He wants a show. He wants to use Mr. McGahn as a prop to spend three hours claiming that Mr. Trump tried to obstruct the Mueller investigation. Yet Mr. Mueller wasn’t obstructed in any way, his copious report was released for all to see, and there was no collusion between Russia and the Trump campaign.”
---
|
---
| “US employers are stepping on the accelerator — and that’s lifting average salaries and fueling record raises across many sectors. For the typical American worker, pay increases could soon surge past 4 percent or 5 percent for the year, according to labor experts,” John Aidan Byrne reports for the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=78b265b946&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “Single-payer. Government-run. Government takeover. One-size-fits-all. Socialist. Each is an accurate description of Democrats’ preferred health-care scheme, proposed in the mislabeled ‘Medicare-for-All Act,’” Reps. Steve Scalise (R-LA) and Greg Walden (R-OR) write in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2e3b5b9812&e=3ee4985eea). “The more Americans learn, the further support sinks for their centralized government health-care system. It will bring delays in access to needed health care. The $32 trillion price tag is so massive it’s hard to wrap your head around.”
---
|
---
| “In a new Hill-HarrisX survey, a majority of registered voters said they support a new Department of Justice inquiry into whether official procedures were followed when the FBI began examining allegations of connections between Donald Trump's 2016 presidential campaign and Russia,” Matthew Sheffield reports for _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e6b8f8a100&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Sixty-two percent of respondents to the May 17-18 survey said they support Attorney General William Barr's” actions—including nearly half of Democratic respondents.
---

name: Kamala Harris
subject: The next phase of our campaign
body:
With the first round of presidential debates fast approaching, we find
ourselves on the cusp of a critical moment in this election. I want to
make sure our people-powered movement is in the strongest position
possible heading into the next phase of our campaign -- which is why my
team and I are setting an ambitious fundraising goal for May.

We need to raise $500,000 online before midnight on Friday, May 31 to make
sure we are in a good spot heading into the first debate next month. I
wouldn’t ask if it wasn’t important: Can I count on you to pitch in
a contribution toward our last
end-of-month deadline before the presidential debates?

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Here's what big, structural change looks like:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
I had a plan for how we could change the rules with a structural fix: A new government agency whose sole mission was to look out for consumers. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fbfd/45c30650/9cfdf1ba/66992757/3798555349/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
Everywhere I go, I talk about how we need to make big, structural change -- to solve the deep, systemic problems we face as a country.

I've seen it work before. Take the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. We had a plan, we got organized, and we turned it into a reality.

The story starts one morning in the 1970s, when I forgot about a few pieces of bread I'd left in the toaster oven -- and nearly set my kitchen on fire.

Back then, our toaster oven had an on-off switch and that was it. At some point, someone had the bright idea of adding a timer and automatic shut-off. This simple change made it a whole lot harder for distracted mothers, or anyone else, to leave it running until it set the kitchen on fire.

Thirty years later, while working on an article about how the government could protect consumers from predatory financial companies, I thought about those old toaster ovens.

By then, **it was all but impossible to buy a toaster that had a one-in-five chance of bursting into flames and burning down your house.** A government agency monitored toasters for basic safety, just like government kept lead paint out of children's toys and rat poison out of medicine.

**But it _was_ possible to refinance a home with a mortgage that had a one-in-five chance of costing a family their home and putting them out on the street.** In fact, it wasn't just possible: Those mortgages were bursting into flames all over the country.

**I had a plan for how we could change the rules with a structural fix: A new government agency whose sole mission was to look out for consumers.** They could serve as the cop on the beat and could make sure that financial companies followed some clear, commonsense rules. No tricks hidden in the fine print, no traps buried in complex legalese.

I wrote my article calling for a new "Financial Product Safety Commission," and I started it by calling out the difference between exploding toasters and exploding mortgages.

After the financial crisis, the plan didn't seem so pie-in-the-sky. So we got organized.

Labor unions like the AFL-CIO and SEIU joined our fight to protect working people. Civil rights organizations like the NAACP and National Council for La Raza jumped in (African American and Latino families had been targeted with some of the very worst subprime mortgages). And groups like the AARP and Consumers Union said this was their fight, too.

Of course, the giant financial institutions were dead set against an agency whose job was to get the tricks and traps out of the fine print of financial products. They spent millions lobbying against financial reform and the new consumer agency.

**It was David-versus-Goliath all the way -- but in 2010, President Obama signed the Dodd-Frank Act into law. Exactly one year later, the new Consumer Financial Protection Bureau was open for business.**

And in the nearly eight years since it's been created, this little agency has forced big financial institutions to return $12 billion directly to people who were cheated.

**We 've done it before, and we can do it again. We'll keep making plans, we'll keep getting organized, and we'll make big, structural change to get Washington working for working people -- not just for the rich and powerful.**

But no one makes it happen on their own. It takes a grassroots movement -- you and me working together, and fighting side by side with people all over the country.

**If you 're ready to keep making change happen, will you support our campaign with a contribution of $3 or whatever you can afford today?**

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: My new economic policy platform: Family Bill of Rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020
body:
America can't thrive unless our families do.
| | |
---
Peter,
America can't thrive unless our families do. But it's _hard_ --not to mention expensive--to raise a happy, healthy family in our country today, and it's disproportionately harder for people of color, LGBTQ Americans, or people with low incomes.
We need to level the playing field in the first, critical years of a child's life, and ease some of the enormous financial burdens that families face when they raise a child.
That's why today, I'm announcing a new economic policy platform: **the Family Bill of Rights.** It's a plan I would enact in my first 100 days as president--a set of five fundamental rights, backed up by bold legislation.
**[Can you add your name today to show your support for my new policy, the Family Bill of Rights? We need to level the playing field so every child can succeed, no matter where they grew up.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9773f4/2ae112ef/682dfe9a/357c74ee/3056171743/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMxMzMtb3QtZ2ZwLW5kIn0=/ "https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/kg2020-family-bill-of-rights?source=jtk3133-ot-gfp-nd")**
My plan lays out a fundamental set of rights to give every child an equal chance to succeed.
It would make it more attainable for everyone to safely and affordably become parents--regardless of income, race or gender--and ensure they have the tools to raise their child in a way that maximizes their future potential.
The Family Bill of Rights includes:
1. **The right to a safe and healthy pregnancy.** America has the highest rate of pregnancy-related deaths in the industrialized world, and black women are 3-4 times more likely to die during or after childbirth than white women. We need to make sure states and hospitals meet higher standards and best practices, and we need to address the shortage of OB-GYNs in rural areas to make it safer to have a child.
2. **The right to give birth or adopt a child, regardless of income, sexual orientation, religion or gender identity.** I would ensure that taxpayer-funded adoption and child welfare agencies cannot discriminate against LGBTQ foster and adoptive families, and I would provide a tax credit to ensure that a family's ability to adopt isn't dependent on wealth. I would also require insurance companies to cover fertility treatments like IVF.
3. **The right to a safe and affordable nursery.** Between diapers, formula, baby food, a crib and clothing, the costs of raising and safely caring for a newborn can quickly add up, and too many families simply can't afford it. I will provide baby bundles--literally a box of supplies--for new parents so that all new families, regardless of income, can start off on the right foot with the supplies they need.
4. **The right to personally care for your loved ones while still getting paid, including care for your child in its infancy.** It's past time we create a national paid family and medical leave insurance program that ensures people can stay in the workforce even when they have a child or need to take care of a sick loved one. And I would ensure every child has the right to medical care up until the age of 18 years old by making CHIP universal.
5. **The right to affordable child care and early education before kindergarten is available publicly.** Every child deserves the same opportunities to learn, grow and reach their potential. I would expand tax credits to make childcare affordable and accessible for working families. And I would help states create truly universal pre-K, professionalize at-home and day care, expand access for children with disabilities, and support the child care workforce.

I'm running for president because I want to fight for everyone's families like I would fight for my own, and that's what this plan is about. Every family in America should be guaranteed these basic, fundamental rights to ensure their and their children's health, opportunity and bright future.
**Sign on to show your support for my new economic policy plan, the Family Bill of Rights, and join me in advocating for every the ability of every family in America to raise a child in a safe, happy and healthy home:**
**[https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/kg2020-family-bill-of-rights](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9773f4/2ae112ef/682dfe9a/357c74ee/3056171743/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMxMzMtb3QtZ2ZwLW5kIn0=/ "https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/s/kg2020-family-bill-of-rights?source=jtk3133-ot-gfp-nd")**
Thank you--I'm really glad to have you on board for this.

Kirsten

clean_body: America can't thrive unless our families do. | | | Peter, America can't thrive unless our families do. But it's hard --not to mention expensive--to raise a happy, healthy family in our country today, and it's disproportionately harder for people of color, LGBTQ Americans, or people with low incomes. We need to level the playing field in the first, critical years of a child's life, and ease some of the enormous financial burdens that families face when they raise a child. That's why today, I'm announcing a new economic policy platform: the Family Bill of Rights. It's a plan I would enact in my first 100 days as president--a set of five fundamental rights, backed up by bold legislation. "" My plan lays out a fundamental set of rights to give every child an equal chance to succeed. It would make it more attainable for everyone to safely and affordably become parents--regardless of income, race or gender--and ensure they have the tools to raise their child in a way that maximizes their future potential. The Family Bill of Rights includes: 1. The right to a safe and healthy pregnancy. America has the highest rate of pregnancy-related deaths in the industrialized world, and black women are 3-4 times more likely to die during or after childbirth than white women. We need to make sure states and hospitals meet higher standards and best practices, and we need to address the shortage of OB-GYNs in rural areas to make it safer to have a child. 2. The right to give birth or adopt a child, regardless of income, sexual orientation, religion or gender identity. I would ensure that taxpayer-funded adoption and child welfare agencies cannot discriminate against LGBTQ foster and adoptive families, and I would provide a tax credit to ensure that a family's ability to adopt isn't dependent on wealth. I would also require insurance companies to cover fertility treatments like IVF. 3. The right to a safe and affordable nursery. Between diapers, formula, baby food, a crib and clothing, the costs of raising and safely caring for a newborn can quickly add up, and too many families simply can't afford it. I will provide baby bundles--literally a box of supplies--for new parents so that all new families, regardless of income, can start off on the right foot with the supplies they need. 4. The right to personally care for your loved ones while still getting paid, including care for your child in its infancy. It's past time we create a national paid family and medical leave insurance program that ensures people can stay in the workforce even when they have a child or need to take care of a sick loved one. And I would ensure every child has the right to medical care up until the age of 18 years old by making CHIP universal. 5. The right to affordable child care and early education before kindergarten is available publicly. Every child deserves the same opportunities to learn, grow and reach their potential. I would expand tax credits to make childcare affordable and accessible for working families. And I would help states create truly universal pre-K, professionalize at-home and day care, expand access for children with disabilities, and support the child care workforce. I'm running for president because I want to fight for everyone's families like I would fight for my own, and that's what this plan is about. Every family in America should be guaranteed these basic, fundamental rights to ensure their and their children's health, opportunity and bright future. Sign on to show your support for my new economic policy plan, the Family Bill of Rights, and join me in advocating for every the ability of every family in America to raise a child in a safe, happy and healthy home: "" Thank you--I'm really glad to have you on board for this. Kirsten | | "" | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. "" | | Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Contributions or gifts to Gillibrand 2020 are not tax-deductible. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Take a moment and add a question:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
We want to hear from more of you as we run to serve in the White House. Listening to all of you share your stories and perspectives shapes my thinking on how we move our country forward and solve the serious challenges ahead. We just opened up our website, social media, and email inboxes to questions. We’re calling it our Town Hall for America, and you can join in right now.

#
Last night’s CNN town hall reminded me of how we started this journey.
From the early days of our Senate campaign, I remember traveling solo from town to town, just asking to meet with anyone who’d spend some time with me. I had the chance to first introduce myself and learn from mayors, farmers, students, doctors, Uber drivers, musicians, artists, teachers, librarians — people from every walk of life.
**Now we want to hear from more of you as we run to serve in the White House.** Listening to all of you share your stories and perspectives shapes my thinking on how we move our country forward to solve the serious challenges ahead.
**But I know not everyone can come to one of our town halls.** And as much as I’d like to personally visit every county in America, as we did in Texas, it may not be possible in the time we have. So we’ve come up with the next best thing.
**We just opened up our website, social media, and email inboxes to questions. We’re calling it our Town Hall for America, and you can join in right now.**
[Can you take a moment to add your question to our Town Hall for America? I’ll do my best to answer as many of these as I can in between stops on the road.](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/town-hall-america/?source=ema20190522-32221&t=1&akid=32221%2E444211%2ERUdHK3)
**[Ask a question](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/town-hall-america/?source=ema20190522-32221&t=2&akid=32221%2E444211%2ERUdHK3)**
In every answer, I will strive to be as honest and thorough as possible. And with every question, my team and I will learn more about what’s important to the people we are seeking to serve in the White House.
When I served on El Paso city council, we held bilingual town halls once a week. We did monthly ones when I got elected to Congress. In our Senate run, we put on more than 328 public town meetings, and we did it in every county of Texas. Now we’ve already visited more counties in Iowa than any other candidate in the field.
We did that because public feedback and input is the heart of our democracy.
[That starts with you adding your question to our Town Hall for America today.](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/town-hall-america/?source=ema20190522-32221&t=3&akid=32221%2E444211%2ERUdHK3)
Thank you for giving me the chance to do this. I'm beyond grateful.
Beto
**[Contribute](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17578?refcode=ema20190522-32221&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=32221%2E444211%2ERUdHK3)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=32221%2E444211%2ERUdHK3). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

name: Dan Baer
subject: need input, not money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
But to win this campaign and get the Senate back fighting for the people, I need to hear from the people...
|
---

| | As I travel around Colorado, voters keep telling me the same thing: They're deeply concerned by how quickly -- and systematically -- Mitch McConnell and his Republican Senate are rolling back decades of progress. What was once the world's greatest deliberative body is now nothing but a way for McConnell to force his wrongheaded agenda on our country. McConnell is stopping critical bills like the Equality Act and anti-corruption legislation from ever reaching the floor. Far-right judges are being confirmed for lifetime appointments at record speed without a single Democratic vote. And McConnell outright refuses to pursue any kind of bipartisanship. I'm just as concerned as you are. That's why I'm running for Senate. **But to win this campaign and get the Senate back fighting for the people, I need to hear from the people ...** **[I just launched a 1-Question Poll where you can tell me exactly what concerns you the most about Mitch McConnell's Republican Senate. Can you please complete it before midnight and share your feedback with me? I want 500 responses.](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826fd/232dfd1/92b90b2/62a16275/181825707/VEsH/ "https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/s/mcconnell-senate-poll-all?source=20190522_em_pet_b1_baer_all")** Thanks, Dan

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: 100 years ago
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Yesterday, we celebrated a landmark anniversary.
| | Peter,

Yesterday, we celebrated a landmark anniversary.

100 days ago, the House of Representatives passed the 19th Amendment, granting women the right to vote and strengthening one of the core principles of democracy.

The right to vote is one that can be easy to take for granted, but it's one that has shaped the course of our nation's collective history, as well as my own personal journey. It is because of the fearless work of courageous suffragettes that I'm here in Washington, serving as the first female representative of VA-07.

In the century since the passage of the 19th Amendment, women have broken into spheres of our society that for too long were solely occupied by men. In the Armed Forces, at boardroom tables, at the top of the charts, and in the halls of Congress, women are writing their own stories and showing future generations what strength and resilience looks like.

**But despite our progress, women across the country are still marginalized.**

Currently, women on average only earn $0.79 for every dollar a man earns -- and the gender pay gap disproportionately affects women of color. Black women are three to four times more likely to die due to pregnancy complications than white women. And women in underserved communities still lack access to the affordable reproductive health care they need.

These aren't the only threats to gender equity. Attacks against women's rights are vast and far-reaching, so let me make it clear: **I pledge to use my agency in Congress to honor the trailblazing women who sacrificed their lives for our freedom and to build a brighter future for women of all backgrounds and identities.**

Peter, thank you for giving me the chance to lead and for giving women a voice in the highest halls of our country. I look forward to the day when our daughters are guaranteed the same freedoms and opportunities as our sons.

_\-- Abigail_ | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/-ag9D6Xy3Est8DQ0teJZzVP7sPXrY1zS6tNk5HoNaYQ=/Se00PrfDFr3NCwz6xb2TQlmTdvwJByJ7znEn3IhbBs4=?refcode=em_na_2019.05.22_b2_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Hoping this guy wins in 2020
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Friends, When I go to work each day, I'm focused on securing wider access to high quality, affordable health care, lowering prescription drug prices, fixing our campaign finance system
|
---

| | Friends, When I go to work each day, I'm focused on securing wider access to high quality, affordable health care, lowering prescription drug prices, fixing our campaign finance system, and supporting our farmers and rural communities. It's an honor to do this work -- but it's hard going sometimes. Right now, there's a lot of important legislation we're having trouble getting through because of who controls the U.S. Senate. **The fact is, we need more Democrats in the Senate if we want to make forward progress. So I 'm writing today to tell you about someone I hope to serve with in 2021 -- a Black Hawk pilot and JAG attorney named James Mackler.** James joined the U.S. Army after September 11th, 2001 because he felt like he had to do more in a time of crisis. As a 30-year-old lawyer, James closed his practice, earned an age waiver, learned to fly a Black Hawk, and became a Screaming Eagle serving in the 101st Airborne Division. James is no stranger to tough fights -- so it's no surprise to me that now he's running to continue serving his country, and all Tennesseans, in the U.S. Senate. [_**Can you make an early investment to help this veteran join me and serve our country in the U.S. Senate?**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2af/18d6a294/4a5804f5/26d6908d/4293799376/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jme190522z?refcode=jme190522z_m&recurring=true") Let me tell you a quick story about James: Wanting to reconnect with his Jewish faith following his deployment, he visited the closest synagogue to his military base. It was there that he found his future wife, Rabbi Shana Goldstein Mackler. They met and fell in love -- and James seldom misses Shabbat services anymore! James knows there are things more important than politics -- like family, duty, and faith -- and in today's polarized environment, we need more people like him in the U.S. Senate. [_**Help James grow his grassroots team with an early investment today.**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2af/18d6a294/4a5804f5/26d6908d/4293799376/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jme190522z?refcode=jme190522z_m&recurring=true") The more people like you who can help out now, the better our chances will be to win in Tennessee next November. Strong, early investments are what really make change happen -- so I'm asking you to step up today and be a part of that change. Thanks so much, -Tim | | | [Donate](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2af/18d6a294/4a5804f5/26d6908d/4293799376/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jme190522z?refcode=jme190522z_m&recurring=true")
